triggers.prompt: Our AI product generates summaries from web sources and users don't trust them. How do we fix this?
triggers.notes: Direct use case: AI-generated outputs from web, trust problem

triggers.prompt: How do we make users trust our AI outputs? We're building a research assistant.
triggers.notes: Exact trigger phrase from description: how do we make users trust our AI outputs

triggers.prompt: We need a confidence scoring framework for our RAG pipeline that pulls from the open web
triggers.notes: Confidence scoring + RAG + open web — direct match

triggers.prompt: Evaluate whether our proposed trust framework for the intelligence dashboard will actually work
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: evaluating whether a proposed trust framework will work

triggers.prompt: We're building an AI that synthesizes information from multiple public data sources — how do we verify accuracy?
triggers.notes: Synthesize + multiple sources + accuracy verification

triggers.prompt: Citations aren't enough for our users — they still don't trust the AI output. What else can we do?
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: citations alone aren't sufficient

triggers.prompt: Design a trust UX for our AI product that extracts intelligence from uncontrolled web sources
triggers.notes: Trust UX + uncontrolled sources — core domain

triggers.prompt: Build an evaluation pipeline for our web-extracted intelligence system
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: building evaluation pipelines for web-extracted intelligence

triggers.prompt: What's the difference between relational and informational trust in AI systems?
triggers.notes: Trust taxonomy question — covered by the skill

triggers.prompt: We're using FActScore but it's not catching all the hallucinations. What else should we look at?
triggers.notes: References specific technique (FActScore) covered by skill

triggers.prompt: Our multi-source RAG system needs better grounding — classic RAG techniques don't work because it's the open web
triggers.notes: Direct trigger: classic RAG grounding doesn't apply, open web corpus

triggers.prompt: Help me design a verification system for AI-generated reports that pull from public data
triggers.notes: Verification + AI-generated + public data

triggers.prompt: What are the common pitfalls when building trust in AI systems? I want a systematic checklist.
triggers.notes: References the 39-issue registry of common pitfalls

triggers.prompt: We need to compare RAGAS vs DeepEval vs HELM for our evaluation pipeline
triggers.notes: References specific tools covered by the skill

triggers.prompt: How do we detect when our AI is citing sources that all say the same thing because they copy each other?
triggers.notes: Source independence detection — core skill concept

triggers.prompt: Review this Python function for bugs
triggers.notes: Code review, not trust evaluation

triggers.prompt: Help me fine-tune a language model on my dataset
triggers.notes: ML training, not trust/accuracy framework

triggers.prompt: Build a RAG pipeline with LangChain and Pinecone for our internal knowledge base
triggers.notes: RAG implementation with controlled corpus — classic RAG, not open web trust

triggers.prompt: Our users don't trust the UI — the buttons are confusing and the layout is bad
triggers.notes: 'Trust' keyword but UX/UI problem, not AI output trust

triggers.prompt: Set up authentication and authorization for our API
triggers.notes: Security trust (authn/authz), not AI output trust

triggers.prompt: Write unit tests for our data pipeline
triggers.notes: Software testing, not AI evaluation

triggers.prompt: Help me implement a chatbot using the Claude API
triggers.notes: Chatbot building, not trust evaluation

triggers.prompt: Optimize my SQL query for the analytics dashboard
triggers.notes: SQL optimization, wrong domain

triggers.prompt: Evaluate this ML model's performance — show me precision, recall, and F1
triggers.notes: Standard ML evaluation, not trust framework design

triggers.prompt: How do I make my website load faster? Users don't trust slow sites.
triggers.notes: 'Trust' keyword but web performance problem

triggers.prompt: Deploy a vector database for semantic search
triggers.notes: Infrastructure setup, not trust design

triggers.prompt: Create a skill that helps with code reviews
triggers.notes: Skill creation, not trust evaluation

triggers.prompt: Our RAG system is hallucinating — how do we detect and prevent this?
triggers.notes: Hallucination detection overlaps with trust evaluation, especially if open-web RAG

triggers.prompt: We need to verify that our AI's answers are actually correct
triggers.notes: Verification/accuracy is core to the skill, even without 'trust' keyword

triggers.prompt: How reliable are AI-generated citations?
triggers.notes: Citation reliability is a subset of trust evaluation

triggers.prompt: Evaluate the accuracy of our internal knowledge base chatbot
triggers.notes: Internal/controlled corpus — classic RAG, not open-web trust. Borderline.

triggers.prompt: I'm worried about bias in our AI system — how do we audit it?
triggers.notes: Bias audit is adjacent but not the same as trust/accuracy framework

triggers.prompt: Should we use Semantic Entropy or ChainPoll for our verification pipeline?
triggers.notes: References specific techniques from the skill's coverage

triggers.prompt: Our AI agent searches the web and summarizes findings — users keep asking 'how do you know this?'
triggers.notes: Web search + trust signal ('how do you know') without using 'trust' keyword

triggers.prompt: We need better error handling when our LLM gives wrong answers
triggers.notes: 'Wrong answers' is close to accuracy, but this is error handling, not framework design

test_cases.prompt: Our AI product pulls from public web sources and users don't trust the outputs. Evaluate our current approach and recommend improvements.
Design a confidence scoring system for our web intelligence platform

assertions.description: Considers how to present confidence to users

test_cases.prompt: Evaluate whether our proposed trust framework will actually work — we show citations and a confidence badge

assertions.description: Identifies gaps in the proposed approach

test_cases.prompt: What are the common pitfalls in building AI trust systems?

assertions.description: Provides multiple items, not just one
References core concepts from the skill

test_cases.prompt: Compare RAGAS, DeepEval, and HELM for evaluating our AI outputs

assertions.description: Actually compares rather than just listing

test_cases.prompt: How do we detect when our AI cites sources that all copy from each other?

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Ask for context about what kind of trust problem

edge_cases.prompt: Evaluate trust for a system that only uses our internal proprietary database — no web sources
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Acknowledge this is controlled corpus, may suggest classic RAG techniques instead

assertions.description: Recognizes controlled corpus vs open web distinction

edge_cases.prompt: We need a trust framework but we only have 2 weeks and no ML engineers
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Provide pragmatic, lightweight recommendations given constraints

edge_cases.prompt: Our CEO wants a 'trust score' badge on every AI output — is that a good idea?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Evaluate the idea critically using trust UX knowledge

assertions.description: Evaluates the idea rather than just implementing it

edge_cases.prompt: Build a trust evaluation for our AI system that handles medical information

assertions.description: Acknowledges the high-stakes nature of medical domain

edge_cases.prompt: We already use RAGAS 0.4 — what's missing from our evaluation?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Build on existing RAGAS setup, identify complementary techniques

assertions.description: Identifies what RAGAS alone doesn't cover
